definition: Measures how well a promotion performed relative to a pre-promotion baseline period. Expressed as a percentage delta comparing promotional sales to the 4-week average before the campaign launched.
The decrease in a metric (e.g. stock level, cost, or units) between two periods. Often used to quantify inventory drawdown or price markdown during a promotion.
The positive percentage change in a metric (e.g. revenue, units_sold) from one period to another. Used to evaluate week-over-week or period-over-period improvement.

The average performance of a metric during a reference period before a promotion begins. Typically calculated as the 4-week rolling average of sales or inventory immediately preceding the promotion start_week.
formula: AVG(metric) OVER the 4 weeks before start_week

definition: The incremental sales or revenue generated by a promotion above the expected baseline. A lift of 20% means the promotion drove 20% more sales than would have been expected without it.
The rate at which inventory is sold and replaced over a given period. A high turnover rate indicates strong sales velocity. Calculated as units sold divided by average stock level.
The likelihood that a SKU will run out of stock within the current or next period. Flagged when stock_level falls below a minimum safety threshold relative to recent sales velocity.
formula: stock_level / AVG(units_sold per week) < safety_threshold (e.g. 2 weeks)

definition: The total measurable effect of a promotional campaign across revenue, units sold, and inventory change. Aggregates lift across all regions and SKUs covered by the campaign during its active window (start_week to end_week).
formula: SUM(revenue during promo) - SUM(expected baseline revenue)

definition: A comparative metric that ranks or scores regions (North, South, East, West) based on a KPI such as total revenue, units sold, or promotion lift during a specified time period.
formula: SUM(revenue) or SUM(units_sold) GROUP BY region ORDER BY metric DESC

definition: The percentage increase in total revenue between two comparable periods (e.g. week-over-week or promotional period vs. baseline). Key indicator of promotion financial success.
